content.problemStatement:
Cherry has a binary matrix AAA consisting of NNN rows and MMM columns. The rows are numbered from 111 to NNN, columns are numbered from 111 to MMM. Element at row iii (111‚ÄČ‚Č§‚ÄČiii‚ÄČ‚Č§‚ÄČNNN) and column jjj (111‚ÄČ‚Č§‚ÄČjjj‚ÄČ‚Č§ MMM) is denoted as AijA_{ij}Aij‚Äč. All elements of AAA are either 000 or 111.

He performs QQQ queries on matrix. Each query is provided by four integers x1x_{1}x1‚Äč, y1y_{1}y1‚Äč, x2x_{2}x2‚Äč, y2y_{2}y2‚Äč which define the rectangle, where (x1x_{1}x1‚Äč,‚ÄČy1y_{1}y1‚Äč) stands for the coordinates of the top left cell of the rectangle, while (x2x_{2}x2‚Äč,‚ÄČy2y_{2}y2‚Äč) stands for the coordinates of the bottom right cell. You need to flip all the bits i.e. (000 to 111, 111 to 000) that are located fully inside the query rectangle.

Finally, print the matrix after performing all the queries.

Note: x1x_{1}x1‚Äč represents the row number while y1y_{1}y1‚Äč represents the column number.

###Input:


The first line of the input contains two integers NNN and MMM ‚ÄĒ the number of rows and the number of columns in the matrix.
Each of the next NNN lines contains a string of length MMM, where the jthj^{th}jth character of ithi^{th}ith line denotes the value of Ai,jA_{i,j}Ai,j‚Äč.
Next line contains an integer QQQ ‚ÄĒ the number of queries.
Then follow QQQ lines with queries descriptions. Each of them contains four space-seperated integers x1x_{1}x1‚Äč, y1y_{1}y1‚Äč, x2x_{2}x2‚Äč, y2y_{2}y2‚Äč ‚ÄĒ coordinates of the up left and bottom right cells of the query rectangle.
###Output:

Print the matrix, in the form of NNN strings, after performing all the queries.

###Constraints


1‚Č§N,M‚Č§10001 \leq N,M \leq 10001‚Č§N,M‚Č§1000
0‚Č§Aij‚Č§10 \leq  A_{ij}  \leq 10‚Č§Aij‚Äč‚Č§1
1‚Č§Q‚Č§1061 \leq Q \leq 10^61‚Č§Q‚Č§106
1‚Č§x1‚Č§x2‚Č§N1 \leq x_{1} \leq x_{2} \leq N1‚Č§x1‚Äč‚Č§x2‚Äč‚Č§N
1‚Č§y1‚Č§y2‚Č§M1 \leq y_{1} \leq y_{2} \leq M1‚Č§y1‚Äč‚Č§y2‚Äč‚Č§M

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1:
After processing the 1st query 1 1 1 1, matrix becomes:
[1000]\begin{bmatrix} 10 \\ 00  \end{bmatrix}[1000‚Äč]
After processing the 2nd query 2 2 2 2, the matrix becomes:
[1001]\begin{bmatrix} 10 \\ 01  \end{bmatrix}[1001‚Äč]
After processing the 3rd query 1 1 2 2, matrix becomes:
[0110]\begin{bmatrix} 01 \\ 10  \end{bmatrix}[0110‚Äč]
We need to output the matrix after processing all queries.
